overview.headline: Our Stories, Our Streets
overview.subhead: An AR walking tour guided by Jacob Fontaine
overview.body: Step into Austin’s Black history through an immersive AR walking tour. At each stop, you’ll unlock a short story that connects place, people, and legacy—bringing the past into view on the streets where it happened.
howItWorks: Choose a stop (or follow the route in order).
Tap Launch AR to open the story experience.
Return here to continue to the next stop.
whatToExpect: Great for self-guided exploration, classes, and community events.

stops.name: Gold Dollar Building
stops.description: A landmark of East Austin’s business and cultural life—where community gathered, built, and sustained itself across generations.

stops.name: Blackland
stops.description: A neighborhood story of home, change, and community identity—told through the places that shaped daily life.

stops.name: Rogers Washington Holy Cross
stops.address: E. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd., Austin, TX 78722
stops.description: A living anchor of faith and community leadership—reflecting how institutions helped hold neighborhoods together.

stops.name: Rosewood Courts
stops.description: A window into everyday life, resilience, and the systems that shaped housing and opportunity in East Austin.

stops.name: Huston Tillotson University
stops.description: A cornerstone of education and leadership—showing how learning institutions powered progress and possibility.

stops.name: Victory Grill
stops.description: A legendary venue where music, culture, and history converged—capturing the sound and spirit of an era.
